Notes: Global financial crisis. Samak/Somchai govts dissolved by court. Abhisit became PM Dec.
Recession. First negative growth since 1997 crisis. Red Shirt protests.
Strong rebound. But Red Shirt crackdown killed 90+. Political instability.
Massive floods. GDP nearly flat. Yingluck became PM Aug.
Post-flood recovery. BOT methodology change added more lenders to debt figure.
Growth slowed. Political crisis began Nov. Household debt rising rapidly.
May coup. Near-zero growth. Political vacuum. Household debt approaching 80% threshold.
Junta stabilization. Household debt crossed 80% 'watchful level' (BOT).
Moderate growth. King Bhumibol died Oct. Brief debt dip.
Best growth since 2012. New constitution in effect.
Strongest growth in years. Pre-election spending.
Growth slowed sharply. Election held Mar. Trade war impact.
COVID crash. Worst contraction since 1997. Debt surged as GDP denominator shrank.
Weak recovery. Household debt hit all-time high ~95.5% in Q1. Protests continued.
Tourism slowly returning. Debt still above 90%.
Disappointing growth. Election May. Srettha became PM Aug. Debt peaked Q4 at 91.3%.
Moderate growth. Srettha removed Aug. Paetongtarn became PM. Debt declining slowly.
Border conflict. Paetongtarn removed. Anutin became PM. Debt at 87.4% Q1, 86.4% Q3.
